heading: Versioned Atlas Administration API Lifecycle
body:
Atlas exposes a new resource version of an existing API (Application Programming Interface) endpoint
when Atlas introduces breaking changes to the current stable version
of the API (Application Programming Interface) endpoint. When Atlas exposes a new resource version,
Atlas automatically deprecates the old resource version. Atlas
automatically deletes the deprecated resource version twelve months
after deprecation. Although Atlas will continue to support the older
resource version for use in your production environment for up to
twelve months, you should migrate to the new version of the
API (Application Programming Interface) endpoint ASAP (as soon as possible). To learn more, see
the example in [Versioning Schema](api-versioning-schema).

Note that with resource-level versioning, each resource in the versioned
Atlas Administration API might introduce a new version independently from
other resources. You have to take action only when the resources you are
utilizing are deprecated.

> **Example:** Suppose Atlas introduces some new resource in
> 2023-06-01, but `/backup` doesn't have any breaking changes and
> stays on 2023-02-01. You must migrate to 2023-06-01 only if you are
> utilizing that resource. If you haven't implemented
> the resource, you don't need to take any action.

Atlas supports deprecated resource version for at least twelve
months until it is removed, which provides you with time to plan and
transition to the new version. You can see changes in each new resource
in the [API changelog](versioned-api-changelog) and details of the
API version in the API specification.
